rows.notes: Centered page heading. No amounts in the row; heading text legible though a small flourish makes the first word slightly uncertain (interpreted as 'Dno').
Clear reading. Pounds/shillings/pence columns unambiguous; the rightmost numerals read as '19 00 00' with strong alignment to the ruling columns.
rows.english_description: Stipend due at the Nativity feast for the rector and scholar.

rows.description: pro Augm. Red. South Newinton & West.
rows.notes: Description legible. Amount clearly '25 00 00' aligned in the pounds/shillings/pence columns; minor ink blotting at the end of the description but not affecting the numbers.
rows.english_description: Augmentation payment relating to the reduced income of South Newington and West.

rows.notes: Text and amount clearly written. Pounds read as '32' with '00 00' in shillings/pence.
rows.english_description: Stipend due at the Annunciation (Lady Day) for the rector and scholar.

rows.description: pro tribus partibus Aug. Red. Rid. Rech.
rows.notes: Pounds and shillings columns align to '19 00'. Pence appears as '10' with a small trailing loop interpreted as 'ob' (halfpenny) → 10½d. Slight ink thinning on the fraction symbol, but alignment supports reading.
rows.english_description: Payment for three parts of the augmentation to a reduced rectory (three‑part share of augmentation).

rows.notes: Description abbreviation (D.B.B.) is somewhat unclear but the monetary columns are well aligned and read '18 06 08'. Chose this reading due to clear digit shapes in the three money columns.
rows.english_description: Stipend at the feast relating to the benefice abbreviated D.B.B.

rows.description: pro 4ta parte Aug. Red. Rid. Rech.
rows.notes: Amount reads as '06 12 03' with a small halfpenny loop after the pence digit interpreted as 3½d. Columns are consistently aligned; fraction symbol is faint but present.
rows.english_description: Payment for the fourth part of the augmentation to a reduced rectory (one‑quarter share of augmentation).

rows.notes: Description contains an abbreviation ('Mue.') that is slightly ambiguous, but the monetary figures '24 05 00' are clearly written and aligned.
rows.english_description: Stipend due at the feast for the benefice abbreviated Mue.

rows.notes: Description legible; amount clearly '16 08 09' with good column alignment and no visible fraction marks.
rows.english_description: Augmentation payment for the reduced rectory of Long Wittenham.

rows.notes: Centered sub-heading introducing a list of names below. No amounts on this row.
Single-name entry with a clear amount '01 17 08'. Alignment of the three money columns is strong and digits are distinct.
rows.english_description: Fine paid by Sadler (Jersey fine).

rows.notes: Name and amount legible; amount reads '03 06 08' with consistent column placement and clear numerals.
rows.english_description: Fine paid by Weston (Jersey fine).

rows.notes: Amount looks like '04 15 07' with a small halfpenny loop after the pence digit interpreted as 7½d. The fraction mark is fainter than whole digits but consistent with other halfpenny marks on the page.
rows.english_description: Fine paid by Wilmot (Jersey fine).

rows.notes: Amount appears as '01 00 01'. The pence digit is small but aligns with the pence column; chosen reading over alternate possibility because the pounds column clearly contains '01'.
rows.english_description: Fine paid by Almond (Jersey fine).

rows.notes: Pounds and shillings read as '07 16'. Pence looks like '06' with a faint fractional loop — interpreted as 6½d. The fraction is faint making the fraction reading somewhat uncertain.
rows.english_description: Fine paid by Jennings (Jersey fine).

rows.notes: Clearly marked total on the right with a currency mark and numerals '179 10 02'. Alignment with the pounds/shillings/pence ruling lines is unambiguous.
Centered section header. No monetary amounts on the header row; heading script is clear.

rows.description: Nov. 4 To mr Wood resid. of South Newin Stip due to yre year there Mic. 1743. & unpd at the last Audit day
rows.notes: Date and long description legible though cramped. Amount '02 02 06' aligns to the money columns clearly; wording irregularities preserved as in the original.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Wood, resident of South Newington, for the stipend due for the year to Michaelmas 1743 which was unpaid at the last audit.

rows.notes: Date '9' and description clear. Amount reads '05 11 06' with clean digits and correct column alignment.
rows.english_description: Payment of a bill to Mr Thomson.

rows.notes: Amount appears as '00 06 00' (6s 0d). The description has a small ink flourish but the numeric columns are clear.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Sadler for four progress expenses (reimbursement for official/progress visits).

rows.notes: Pounds read as '33' which is larger than most nearby items; digits are legible but unusually large value makes it slightly suspect. Alignment is consistent with pounds column and shillings/pence read as '08 00'.
rows.english_description: Payment made to Mr Wilmot (purpose not specified).

rows.notes: Small sub-line under the Wilmot payment. Amount reads '00 07 06'. The pence digits are fairly clear; this appears to be a related sub-item (wine) and aligns to the money columns.
rows.english_description: Payment for wine (purchase of wine).

rows.notes: Description 'Mrs Jaques' is somewhat ambiguous (handwriting), and the amount is less clear than some lines; read as '05 01 04' from alignment of digits. Slight ink bleed on the pence makes exact pence digit uncertain but 4d fits the visible strokes.
rows.english_description: Payment of Mrs Jaques's bill (unspecified household or supply bill).

rows.description: 30 to Coffee on y Audit day
rows.notes: Short entry and amount '00 02 08' are clear; the words 'Coffee on y Audit day' are legible and the money columns well aligned.
rows.english_description: Payment for coffee purchased on the audit day.

rows.notes: Amount reads '00 11 00' clearly. Name 'Mr Franklin' legible; small ink smudge does not affect the numbers.
rows.english_description: Payment of Mr Franklin's bill (unspecified).

rows.notes: The left-side date/notation ('9 2') is compact and a little ambiguous; amount reads '00 12 06' (12s 6d). Chose this over other placements because digits line up clearly in the money columns.
rows.english_description: Subscription payment to Mr Lye.

rows.description: 2 to Mr Brooks for bringing Tablone Rent
rows.notes: Date '2' and description legible though cramped. Monetary columns read '00 02 06'. The phrase 'Tablone Rent' is faint but plausible; amount digits are unambiguous.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Brooks for bringing in the Tablone rent (rent collection service).

rows.description: 12 pd Newmans Land tax to day 1743
rows.notes: Amount reads '00 18 08' clearly and aligns with the date '12' and the description 'Newmans Land tax'. Handwriting in description is slightly compressed but legible.
rows.english_description: Payment of Newman's land tax for 1743.

rows.notes: Short entry; amount '00 03 00' is clear. The last word of description reads 'Junins' (binding of something) — legible though archaic spelling.
rows.english_description: Payment for binding (likely of Junius or similar volumes).

rows.description: 24 to ys Cook his years Act & of Act Dinner
rows.notes: Description is somewhat abbreviated ('ys Cook', 'years Act'); monetary amount reads '04 14 04' with clear digits. The wording is preserved as in the original line.
rows.english_description: Payment to the college cook for his year's act and the act dinner (annual cook's payment).

rows.description: to Mr Hillow per for Recover? Rundle's Battley
rows.notes: Description contains several unclear names/words ('Hillow', 'Rundles Battley') — these are transcribed as they appear. Amount reads '00 13 04' but both a slightly faded ink and unusual wording reduce confidence.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Hillow for recovering Rundle's battels (collection of Rundle's outstanding college charges).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Hillow for recovering Rundle's battels (collection of Rundle's outstanding college charges), amounting to £0 13s 4d.
